# 📘F3 Nation Slack App

# Standard Operating Procedures (SOP)

Welcome to the SOP Master Document for navigating the **F3 Nation Slack App**. This resource is designed to provide clear, step-by-step guidance for key functions within the app. Each tab within this document contains a dedicated SOP to help you perform essential tasks with confidence and consistency.

## 🔗Quick Access to SOPs:

* [**Installing F3 Nation**](?tab=t.8izebhckznu8)  
* [**Submitting a Backblast**](?tab=t.0)  
* [**Signing Up to Q**](?tab=t.nmu55z13mu13)  
* [**Querying the Database**](?tab=t.p27e9t3ri388)  
* [**Starfish Instructions**](?tab=t.n94uici1lrt2)  
* [**Frequently Asked Questions**](?tab=t.5anzgzo1iu9)

Use these SOPs to ensure smooth and accurate interaction with the F3 Nation App. Whether you're a seasoned user or just getting started, this guide will help you stay on track and lead effectively.

# So wait… why a new app?\!? What was wrong with the old ones?

Great question, glad you asked\! There are a ton of reasons, but to summarize a few of them:

* **Better support for the "plant" part of F3** \- we're in a starfish era of F3, the plant era. One challenge of that is local tech support. Whereas it's easy to find 1-2 tech monkeys in a whole metro area, it's a lot harder as that metro starfishes. Same thing with small towns, etc. Our goal with the tech tools we've been working on over the last 12 months (including this app, but also the new F3 Nation Map, Region Pages ([All Regions](https://regions.f3nation.com/)), etc.) is to **drastically lessen the tech burden** on new regions so they can focus on the real mission of F3 \- **reaching sad clowns**. While the "legacy" suite of Slack tools are better than no tools, they still require someone pretty tech savvy for regions to utilize  
* **Unification of effort and vision** \- the legacy suite of F3 tools was sort of a jumbled mess. We had several different owners and tools doing similar things without any sort of official support. We had stuff hosted by different providers that were being paid by individual HIMs, everything was screwed if a key person dropped out, etc. This all just led to growing tech debt that is confusing to regions and harder for us to maintain over time. The new tools and database are now under an "official" F3 umbrella with unified goals and vision. For example, the F3 Nation app now syncs with the new F3 Map, [f3near.me](http://f3near.me), Region Pages, etc.  
* **Modernization** \- there was a lot that we felt like could be improved upon with the legacy stack of tools, and especially with the database rebuild, it makes a lot more sense to rebuild the tools from the ground up rather than trying to adapt the legacy tools. While we were at it, we took the time to build out a lot of synergies and improvements we think you’ll appreciate

We want to make something clear, though... other than keeping maps up to date, there is no requirement that a region use any of this \- they’re only readily available resources regions can utilize if they want (and we think you will\!). A key aspect of F3 is "freed to lead", and so we totally want to enable regions to do their own thing if they so choose. To that end, all of our code is open source and easy to fork (either on [F3Nation-Community · GitHub](https://github.com/F3Nation-Community/) for legacy stuff, [F3 Nation · GitHub](https://github.com/F3-Nation) for the new stack), and we're even building up an API that regions can have their custom apps tap into the new db. If you’d like to help develop the Nation tools, we would LOVE to have you\!

# How does migration from paxminer / slackblast work?

![][image1]

# Feature Comparison / Feature Status

The new Slackbot is not a 1-to-1 replacement of the old suite of bots. Attempts were made to transfer over all of the main features. Some are still in development. This table will help you understand what exists, what is planned to exist, and what might be left behind. If there are any features you don’t see here that you want to know about, please post a question on \#f3nation-slackbot in the F3 Nation Slack Workspace and ask.

**New stuff not in the legacy apps:**

* Synchronization with maps / f3near.me / Region Pages  
* Synergy between scheduling and attendance tracking. This allows things like preblast and backblast reminders that are specific to each event  
* Automated preblasts for guys that forget to post them on time  
* One button clicks for a lot of things (taking a Q from the Q lineups, backblast reminder button, etc)  
* Support from the official F3 dev team  
* The unified DB opens up the possibility of better cross-region reporting like [PAX Vault](https://pax-vault.f3nation.com/) and functionality like DR PAX tagging

**Features in legacy apps:**

| Legacy App | Feature | F3 Nation App Status |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| PAXminer | Database of users, AOs, Backblasts, and attendance | Complete. Workouts integrated with [map.f3nation.com](http://map.f3nation.com)  |
|  | Scraping of non-Slackblast backblasts | This will not be brought into the new app. All backblasts must be submitted via the bot menus. |
|  | Monthly reports | There is currently a basic Post and Q trend that is posted monthly. This currently is not planned to be expanded. PAX should leverage [PAX Vault](https://pax-vault.f3nation.com) instead.  |
|  | Direct modifications to database data | PAX will no longer have access to directly edit the master database. All interactions will be done through the map, slackbot or other apps. |
|  | Querying database access  | Complete. [Request access to our Google Group](https://groups.google.com/u/3/a/f3nation.com/g/f3-data-analytics) to gain access to our BigQuery console |
| Slackblast | Submit backblasts via a Slack form | Complete. Backblasts can be initiated via the slash command, a shortcut, or interacting with the new calendar. |
|  | Welcome messages | Complete. Mostly the same as it was |
|  | Email backblasts | Complete. Mostly the same as it was |
|  | Strava integration | Not currently working |
| QSignups | Sign up for Qing | Complete. PAX can sign up to Q via the new calendar. Additionally, Slack Admins can assign Qs. |
| Weaselbot | Achievements | In progress. The hope is that in addition to standardized achievements, we will also have functionality for customizable achievements. |
|  | Kotter reports | Integrated into [PAX Vault](https://pax-vault.f3nation.com/), may still build into Slack |

# 🙏Thank Yous

Moneyball (WashMo) for putting in countless hours, first on Slackblast and QSignups and then on the F3 Nation App. His vision and borderline addiction has had, and will continue to have, a monumental impact on getting guys off their computers and back out into the gloom.

Beaker (STL) for kicking this whole thing off when he stood up PAXminer \- and for funding it for so many years. (Sorry to all the other Beakers in the Nation that kept having people message them on Slack.)

Sumo (Chicago) for taking on Weaselbot and enhancing it to acknowledge those who are accelerating and helping to find those that are wandering off.

LCD (The Junction \- Wentzville, MO) for spearheading documentation and video tutorials to help whoever is reading this right now.
